meanings.definition: be shown or be found to be
examples: She proved to be right
The medicine turned out to save her life
She turned up HIV positive

meanings.definition: prove to be in the result or end
examples: It turns out that he was right

meanings.definition: produce quickly or regularly, usually with machinery
examples: This factory turns out saws
How will the game turn out?

meanings.definition: come, usually in answer to an invitation or summons
examples: How many people turned out that evening?
The apple tree bore delicious apples this year
The unidentified plant bore gorgeous flowers

meanings.definition: put out or expel from a place
examples: The unruly student was excluded from the game

meanings.definition: come and gather for a public event
examples: Hundreds of thousands turned out for the anti-war rally in New York

meanings.definition: outfit or equip, as with accessories
examples: The actors were turned out lavishly
These birds can splay out their toes
ballet dancers can rotate their legs out by 90 degrees

meanings.definition: cause to stop operating by disengaging a switch
examples: Turn off the stereo, please
cut the engine
turn out the lights

meanings.definition: get up and out of bed
examples: I get up at 7 A.M. every day
They rose early
He uprose at night
